name: Jabberwock
primary_sources: Through the Looking-Glass by Lewis Carroll
The Annotated Alice by Martin Gardner

distinctive_features: long scaly neck and tail of a sauropod

domain_specific_visual.Victorian_influence: reflects Victorian obsession with natural history

domain_specific_functional.nonsense_poetry: embodies the whimsical and nonsensical nature of the poem

relationships.description: The Jabberwock is slain by the protagonist in the poem.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the whimsical and nonsensical elements of Carroll's work
primary_sources: Through the Looking-Glass by Lewis Carroll
The Annotated Alice by Martin Gardner

contrastive_constraints: NOT a creature from traditional mythology
